world.title: The Earth's inner core grows by roughly a millimetre a year as molten iron freezes onto it, and that slow crystallisation releases the energy that stirs the outer core and keeps the planet's magnetic
world.description: Earth's inner core grows by about a millimetre a year as molten iron freezes onto its surface, and the latent heat and buoyant fluids released by that slow crystallisation drive the convection in the outer core that generates the planet's magnetic field.
